title: FileEditTool: replace_all parameter unsupported in OC (silent flag drop)
description:
## Summary

CC's FileEditTool supports a `replace_all: boolean` parameter that, when true, replaces every occurrence of `old_string` with `new_string`. OC's `execute_edit_file` (`src/tools/file/edit.rs:84-95`) unconditionally rejects multi-occurrence matches regardless of `replace_all`. The flag is silently dropped: callers passing `replace_all=true` get the same multi-occurrence rejection as if they passed it false or omitted it.

## CC Reference

- `tools/FileEditTool/FileEditTool.ts:329-343` (validateInput, counts matches and gates on `replace_all`)
- `tools/FileEditTool/FileEditTool.ts:482-488` (`getPatchForEdit({ replaceAll: replace_all })` → `file.replaceAll(actualOldString, new_string)`)

## Gap

`src/tools/file/edit.rs:84-95` — count > 1 always errors; `replace_all` field is not threaded into the multi-occurrence path. Single-occurrence with `replace_all=true` silently ignores the flag (works only because count=1 already uses `replacen(_,_,1)`).

## Parent Spec Issue

Discovered during Phase 1 spec extraction for #525.
